commentaries.author: Douay Rheims 1609
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 2 Chronicles 11:8 And Gath, and Mareshah, and Ziph, Douay Rheims 1609 But the daughter of Pharao he removed from the city of David, into the house, which he had built for her. For the king said: My wife shall not dwell in the house of David the king of Israel, (a) because it is sanctified: because the ark of our Lord is entered into it. (a) Salomon was yet so far from communicating with Infidels, that he suffered not his wife (an infidel) to be present where the ark of God had been placed.
